author: @another3997
text: Make it in to a pedal powered recumbent quad, with motor assist. Add a boom at the front, add a chainset and run a chain to a front wheel.  Whatever happens, unless you like interfacing with immovable objects, the steering geometry will need some work. 😉

author: @MichaelHonsinger
text: If you do finish the trike I hope you'll consider putting the single wheel in back. It will greatly add to the stability. Buckminster Fuller's Dymaxion Car had that configuration for that reason. There is a film of him circling a traffic cop:  the front end is facing the cop the whole time he is circling him.

author: @montynorth3009
text:
This reminds me of a simple cart that my father built for me as a child.
He used a wooden plank with a legless chair on top and a set of pram wheels fixed to cross planks, the front one pivoted for steering either with my feet or a rope loop.
Electrification would have been brilliant.
There were no brakes.

author: @edkoetsier339
text: Pity Builders Board is not available in Australia... We are really slow to uptake new products. Especially electric cars. Would you believe they are classified as Luxury Vehicles and receive MORE tax and duties...

author: @docink6175
text: I'm with you, design as you build but we are a minority it seems, so many have to research things for months, draw out their ideas on cad or whatever before they even start to gather materials and heaven forbid something doesnt work, they cant switch gears and use a different material without researching it forst, adding a wheel? ooh no we need to calculate how that will effect ...... lol

author: @401ksolar
text: I was introduced to the synboard l in 1998, some builders we're building a bank and used synboard for the soffits they showed me how to use a small belt sander with old sandpaper on the edges and you can make the edges smooth just like you can on the top that's actually how they make it is panel Sanders with dull sandpaper LOL it is a burnished finish the duller the sandpaper the better.

author: @bigofano1794
text: There are many good videos on utube concerning steering geometry on diy recumbent trikes. I found simple explanations for a very complex subject.

replies.author: @bigofano1794
replies.text: @McRootbeer five years ago I designed and built a recumbent. I watched many videos, one of the utube channels I found helpful was minibul channel. I don't remember the videos, just Google recumbent trike design, recumbent trike steering geometry. There are many good resources with very good description of design parameters. These principles are applicable to cars, gocarts, bicycles, motorcycles most any thing that uses a wheel to steer it. The bicycle folks certainly offered the best info for understanding the principles. Good luck to ya, Neighbor.

author: @peterjames2117
text: Hi Rob I bought some conductive ink and graphite flake from your website. I have tried contacting you several times via your website to ask whether a materials safety data sheet is available for the ink or any similar safety information as I cannot use the ink in our lab without this information. Please can you let me know. Peter Walters, Oxford University.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @Beeeeeeeeeee
text: Oh dear, You might want to add suspension if you're making it electric. I've built a quad before without suspension, couldn't corner at all without flying out of the seat... Really had to grip the handlebars tight.
